title: AcyclicNonCollidersBlockable
lean_code_with_comments:
/-- claim_3_26 (theorem 1/3, walk-level simplification): on a
walk $\pi$ in an acyclic CDMG, the existence of a *blockable*
non-collider position in $C$ is equivalent to the existence of
*any* non-collider position in $C$. The forward direction is
the definition `IsBlockableNonColliderAt → IsNonColliderAt` (via
`.1`); the backward direction is claim_3_20
(`isBlockableNonColliderAt_of_isNonColliderAt_of_isAcyclic`)
applied to the witness. -/
theorem exists_isBlockableNonColliderAt_and_mem_iff_exists_isNonColliderAt_and_mem_of_isAcyclic
    (hG : G.IsAcyclic) {v w : α} (π : Walk G v w) (C : Set α) :
    (∃ k, π.IsBlockableNonColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∈ C) ↔
      (∃ k, π.IsNonColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∈ C) := by
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨k, hBlock, hMem⟩
    exact ⟨k, hBlock.1, hMem⟩
  · rintro ⟨k, hNon, hMem⟩
    exact ⟨k, isBlockableNonColliderAt_of_isNonColliderAt_of_isAcyclic hG π hNon, hMem⟩

/-- claim_3_26 (theorem 2/3, $\sigma$-blocked-level simplification):
under acyclicity, the $C$-$\sigma$-blocked predicate of def_3_17 is
equivalent to its *simplified* form in which the second existential
disjunct weakens from "*blockable* non-collider in $C$" to "(any)
non-collider in $C$". The collider disjunct is unchanged. The LN
prose-form of the claim, the bundled corollary of theorem 1. -/
theorem isSigmaBlocked_iff_simplified_of_isAcyclic
    (hG : G.IsAcyclic) {v w : α} (π : Walk G v w) (C : Set α) :
    π.IsSigmaBlocked C ↔
      (∃ k, π.IsColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∉ G.AncSet C) ∨
      (∃ k, π.IsNonColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∈ C) := by
  rw [Walk.isSigmaBlocked_iff]
  exact or_congr Iff.rfl
    (exists_isBlockableNonColliderAt_and_mem_iff_exists_isNonColliderAt_and_mem_of_isAcyclic
      hG π C)

/-- claim_3_26 (theorem 3/3, $i\sigma$-separation lift): under
acyclicity, $A$ is $i\sigma$-separated from $B$ given $C$ in $G$
iff every walk from a node in $A$ to a node in $G.J ∪ B$
satisfies the *simplified* blocking condition -- a collider
outside $\Anc^G(C)$, or *any* non-collider in $C$. The bridge
identity motivating the LN's introduction of *$id$-separation*
(def_3_20, subsection 3.4) as a more economical surface on
acyclic CDMGs. -/
theorem isISigmaSeparated_iff_simplified_of_isAcyclic
    (hG : G.IsAcyclic) (A B C : Set α) :
    G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C ↔
      ∀ ⦃v w : α⦄, v ∈ A → w ∈ G.J ∪ B → ∀ (π : Walk G v w),
        (∃ k, π.IsColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∉ G.AncSet C) ∨
        (∃ k, π.IsNonColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∈ C) := by
  rw [CDMG.isISigmaSeparated_iff]
  refine forall_congr' (fun v => forall_congr' (fun w =>
    imp_congr_right (fun _ => imp_congr_right (fun _ =>
      forall_congr' (fun π => ?_)))))
  exact Walk.isSigmaBlocked_iff_simplified_of_isAcyclic hG π C
lean_code_without_comments:
theorem exists_isBlockableNonColliderAt_and_mem_iff_exists_isNonColliderAt_and_mem_of_isAcyclic
    (hG : G.IsAcyclic) {v w : α} (π : Walk G v w) (C : Set α) :
    (∃ k, π.IsBlockableNonColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∈ C) ↔
      (∃ k, π.IsNonColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∈ C) := by
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨k, hBlock, hMem⟩
    exact ⟨k, hBlock.1, hMem⟩
  · rintro ⟨k, hNon, hMem⟩
    exact ⟨k, isBlockableNonColliderAt_of_isNonColliderAt_of_isAcyclic hG π hNon, hMem⟩

theorem isSigmaBlocked_iff_simplified_of_isAcyclic
    (hG : G.IsAcyclic) {v w : α} (π : Walk G v w) (C : Set α) :
    π.IsSigmaBlocked C ↔
      (∃ k, π.IsColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∉ G.AncSet C) ∨
      (∃ k, π.IsNonColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∈ C) := by
  rw [Walk.isSigmaBlocked_iff]
  exact or_congr Iff.rfl
    (exists_isBlockableNonColliderAt_and_mem_iff_exists_isNonColliderAt_and_mem_of_isAcyclic
      hG π C)

theorem isISigmaSeparated_iff_simplified_of_isAcyclic
    (hG : G.IsAcyclic) (A B C : Set α) :
    G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C ↔
      ∀ ⦃v w : α⦄, v ∈ A → w ∈ G.J ∪ B → ∀ (π : Walk G v w),
        (∃ k, π.IsColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∉ G.AncSet C) ∨
        (∃ k, π.IsNonColliderAt k ∧ π.nodeAt k ∈ C) := by
  rw [CDMG.isISigmaSeparated_iff]
  refine forall_congr' (fun v => forall_congr' (fun w =>
    imp_congr_right (fun _ => imp_congr_right (fun _ =>
      forall_congr' (fun π => ?_)))))
  exact Walk.isSigmaBlocked_iff_simplified_of_isAcyclic hG π C
lean_explanation:
The file formalises claim 3.26 as three layered statements about the simplification of the $i\sigma$-separation blocking condition under acyclicity. The implicit type variable `α : Type*` is the ambient vertex carrier (the silent universal of every CDMG definition in the chapter). The graph `G : CDMG α` is an instance of the chapter-3 mixed graph structure (def_3_5); the hypothesis `hG : G.IsAcyclic` corresponds to the LN's "if $G$ is acyclic" precondition.

In theorem 1, `(π : Walk G v w)` is a $G$-walk from $v$ to $w$ (def_3_13), and `(C : Set α)` is the conditioning set $C$ from the LN. The two sides of the iff existentially quantify over a position index `k` along the walk: `π.IsBlockableNonColliderAt k` corresponds to "$v_k$ is a blockable non-collider on $\pi$" (def_3_16); `π.IsNonColliderAt k` to "$v_k$ is a non-collider on $\pi$" (def_3_15); and `π.nodeAt k ∈ C` to "$v_k \in C$".

Theorem 2 reshapes the simplification into the LN's $\sigma$-blocking prose form: `π.IsSigmaBlocked C` (def_3_17) on the LHS, and on the RHS an explicit disjunction of "collider outside $\mathrm{Anc}^G(C)$" with "any non-collider in $C$". The Mathlib idiom `G.AncSet C` is the ancestral set $\mathrm{Anc}^G(C)$.

Theorem 3 lifts the simplification all the way up to $i\sigma$-separation (def_3_18): `G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C` on the LHS, and on the RHS the explicit three-nested universal `∀ ⦃v w⦄, v ∈ A → w ∈ G.J ∪ B → ∀ (π : Walk G v w), ...` whose body is the same simplified blocking disjunction. The strict-implicit binders `⦃v w : α⦄` are Lean's idiom for arguments inferred from later hypothesis types: callers supply `(h hv hw π)` and Lean reconstructs `v` and `w` from `hv : v ∈ A` and `hw : w ∈ G.J ∪ B`. `G.J ∪ B` is the union of $G$'s input vertices ($J$) with $B$, mirroring the LN's "$J \cup B$" endpoint set in def_3_18.

Each theorem is proven by a short straight-line tactic chain (`constructor` / `or_congr` / `forall_congr'` / `imp_congr_right`) that pivots through the previous level via the corresponding `_iff` lemma; theorem 1 invokes claim_3_20's `isBlockableNonColliderAt_of_isNonColliderAt_of_isAcyclic` on the backward direction, and theorems 2 and 3 are straight-line corollaries.
design_choices:
**Iff, not a one-way replace.** The simplification is stated as a biconditional at all three layers. The LN's wording -- *"can be simplified to"* -- reads cleanly in both directions, and downstream consumers need both: rewriting an $i\sigma$-separation goal into the simpler $id$-separation-style form (forward), and lifting a witness from the simpler form back into the $i\sigma$-separation surface for theorems stated in def_3_17 vocabulary (backward). An `Iff` captures both uses without forcing two separate theorems.

**Three theorems at three abstraction layers, not one bundled statement.** The simplification has a natural layered structure: walk-existential level (theorem 1), per-walk $\sigma$-blocking level (theorem 2), and full $i\sigma$-separation level (theorem 3). Each layer corresponds to a different downstream consumer profile -- proofs that reason directly about non-collider witnesses, proofs that match the LN's surface prose, and proofs that switch between $i\sigma$- and (later) $id$-separation surfaces in subsection 3.4. A single bundled theorem would force every consumer to manually unfold two or three quantifier layers before applying the simplification; the layered presentation makes each call site a one-step rewrite. The three theorems are inter-derivable in straight-line chains, so the layering adds no mathematical content -- only proof ergonomics. The alternative of stating only the top-level theorem 3 and forcing consumers at theorems 1 and 2's level to peel quantifiers each time was rejected for this reason.

**Strict-implicit binders `⦃v w : α⦄` on theorem 3 mirror `IsISigmaSeparated`.** def_3_18's definition uses strict-implicit binders for the endpoint witnesses so callers can write `(h hv hw π)` and have Lean reconstruct $v$ and $w$ from the hypothesis types. The iff preserves the exact same binder shape on its RHS, so the simplification reads as a literal surface substitution: anywhere a caller could use `IsISigmaSeparated`, the simplified form drops in with the same call shape and no extra binder bookkeeping.

**A new file rather than extending claim_3_20's `AcyclicNonCollidersBlockable.lean`.** The two rows share a title because the LN reuses the first sentence verbatim across both, but the *novel* content here is the simplification of the $i\sigma$-separation blocking condition -- not the underlying "all non-colliders are blockable" fact that claim_3_20 already provides. The new file name `AcyclicISigmaSeparationSimplification.lean` disambiguates at the filename level and reflects the row's genuine mathematical contribution, while leaving claim_3_20's existing Lean file unmodified.
